title: Set a default log driver if none is specified
body:
Not having a default broke play kube. Rather than requiring a caller to add a log driver, let's set a logical default in the library

note: I considered fixing it on the play kube side, but instead opted for this. If that option is preferred though, it's a single line of code. Hard to tell who should be responsible for the default here.

Fixes: https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/3157#issuecomment-500104440

comments.body: I also think this could be fixed by having the create config creation a bit more standardized, but that requires lots ripping apart and putting together of existing code

LGTM
/approve

@giuseppe @baude @TomSweeneyRedHat @mheon @vrothberg PTAL
@Fodoj PTAL

I don't like setting it on empty string - setting it as part of initial
container creation, and only passing the option of a non empty driver was
specified, sounds more correct.

Excuse my ignorance, what's PTAL? (please test AL) ? :)
Please Take A Look == PTAL  Sorry @Fodoj
What is the way to specify log driver interactively? `podman play kube --help` didn't give me an answer. Perhaps extra documentation around it would be helpful.

podman run or podman create allow you to specify the log-driver.  If the user created the containers with an alternative log-driver, then the yaml should reflect that.  But I thought the original bug was just that the yaml did not specify any log-driver.
@rhatdan the issue was log driver isn't really specified in the yaml, and before we always set it to kubernetes logging. Now that there are options, we have to set the default correctly somewhere.
@mheon you're right, that solution is much better (we already set a default down the stack anyway)
@mheon @rhatdan @baude PTAL
LGTM

LGTM. Nice and simple.
/gltm

Oh.
/lgtm
@mheon @haircommander @baude, anything against backporting that to 1.3? CentOS 7 users asked on IRC. I don't know when 1.4+ would hit CentOS 7.
Note that the motivation for CentOS is mostly to get play kube work with volumes.

Hi, I am the CentOS 7 user mentioned by **vrothberg**.

I've just backported the commit from this merge request locally to v1.3.2. A local build results in
```
_build/src/github.com/containers/libpod/pkg/spec/createconfig.go:324:29: undefined: libpod.WithLogDriver
```

I expect this is not part of v1.3 though.

Furthermore I want to clarify that my motivation is to use
``podman generate kube ...``

which results in
```
Error: unable to find mount source
```

From #2963 I got the impression that this issue has already been fixed in v1.4 which is unfortunately not available in CentOS 7. Nevertheless I don't think this merge request here addresses my problem.

We're shipping a 1.4.x (I think 1.4.2) in 7.6.6, so a fix is definitely coming.

We used to have a repo shipping more recent versions for 7 that you could use in the meantime - @lsm5 does that still exist?

Meanwhile I've checked podman v1.4.4-1.el7 from cbs.centos.org which resolves my problem. Looking forward for an official rpm in the future.

Thanks a lot guys.
